name: Nafanua
description: Nafanua is a significant figure in Samoan mythology, revered as a warrior goddess. Her origins are traced back to the village of Falealupo on the island of Savai'i. According to legend, she was the daughter of Saveasi'uleo, the ruler of Pulotu, the Samoan underworld. Nafanua is known for her strength, courage, and strategic prowess in battle. She played a crucial role in liberating Samoa from Tongan rule, leading the Samoan warriors to victory. Her powers included exceptional combat skills, the ability to inspire her troops, and a connection to the spiritual realm. Nafanua is considered a symbol of Samoan independence, female empowerment, and the spirit of resistance against oppression. She is often depicted with a warrior's club (an 'Anava) and is associated with justice and protection. Her legacy continues to inspire generations of Samoans, reminding them of their strength and resilience. She is seen as a protector of the Samoan people and a symbol of hope in times of conflict.
subtitle: Samoan Warrior Goddess of Independence

keyMyths.title: Birth and Training in Pulotu
keyMyths.description: Nafanua was born and raised in Pulotu, the underworld, under the tutelage of her father, Saveasi'uleo. She was trained in the arts of war and strategy.

keyMyths.title: Liberation of Samoa from Tonga
keyMyths.description: Nafanua led the Samoan warriors against the Tongan overlords, utilizing her strategic brilliance and combat skills. Her victory secured Samoa's independence.

keyMyths.title: Establishment of the Fa'alupega
keyMyths.description: After her victory, Nafanua played a role in establishing the Fa'alupega, the traditional order of Samoan titles and governance, ensuring peace and stability.

sources.author: Margaret Mead
sources.description: Anthropological study providing context to Samoan culture.
sources.text: Anthropological study providing context to Samoan culture.
